name: COUNTS CORNER 2

surface_od_m.qualifier: as written: 'Top of borehole 38.25 m. AOD'

units: metres, transcribed verbatim from a depth-range narrative log; depth = base of interval below ground level

intervals.verbatim: Broken sandstone cores with some siltstone bands, upper part may be quarry debries and fill.
annotations: upper part may be quarry debris and fill (possible made ground over broken bedrock sandstone); classification of the upper part is uncertain

intervals.verbatim: Dark mudstone with plants interbedded with siltstone and sandstone bands. Cores show reaming. At 11.25 m broken mussel shells in ironstone Low Main Shell Bed.
annotations: marine band marker: Low Main Shell Bed (broken mussel shells in ironstone) at 11.25 m

intervals.verbatim: Coal bright cleaty only fragments seen. Lower leaf of Low Main Seam (J2).
annotations: only fragments seen; named lower leaf of Low Main Seam (J2)

intervals.verbatim: Soft light grey coloured seatearth clay.
Interbedded greybeds, siltstone and mudstone with plants.
annotations: interbedded 'greybeds', siltstone and mudstone; classified siltstone as the leading nameable lithology

intervals.verbatim: Black mudstone with many plant fossils.
Interbedded mudstone, siltstone and fine grained sandstone.
annotations: interbedded with siltstone and fine grained sandstone

intervals.verbatim: Mudstone with mussel shells,. Brass Thill Shell Bed.
annotations: marine band marker: Brass Thill Shell Bed (mussel shells)
stray comma-period in original as written

intervals.verbatim: Mudstone with ironstone nodules and plants - Neuropteris.
annotations: ironstone nodules; plant fossil Neuropteris named

intervals.verbatim: Coal and seatearth clay, poor core recovery. Top Brass Thill Coal (K1).
annotations: mixed coal and seatearth clay, poor core recovery; named Top Brass Thill Coal (K1)

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded mudstone, siltstone and fine grained sandstone. Frequent mussel shells at base - lower leaf of Brass Thill Shell Bed.
annotations: frequent mussel shells at base = lower leaf of Brass Thill Shell Bed

intervals.verbatim: Core lost by reaming. Position of the Bottom Brass Thill Coal, upper leaf (K2).
annotations: core lost by reaming; lithology inferred from position of Bottom Brass Thill Coal upper leaf (K2)

intervals.verbatim: Trough cross bedded fine grained sandstone interbedded with siltstone and mudstone.
Dark mudstone with many plants, siltstone and fine grained sandstone bands.
Coal only 0.05 m fragmentary core. Botton Brass Thill Coal, lower leaf (K3).
annotations: only 0.05 m fragmentary core recovered over the 0.25 m interval; named Bottom Brass Thill Coal lower leaf (K3)

intervals.verbatim: Interbedded mudstone, siltstone and sandstone.
Light coloured fine and medium grained cross bedded sandstone.
Light coloured, fine and medium grained sandstone with partings and bands of siltstone and mudstone.
White and buff fine and medium grained micaceous sandstone with whispy iron stained bands.
Interbedded bands of fine grained sandstone, siltstone and mudstone. Many plant fossils.
annotations: interbedded fine grained sandstone, siltstone and mudstone; classified sandstone as leading lithology

intervals.verbatim: Dark mudstone with siltstone and sandstone bands.
Bright cleaty coal, Hutton (L).
Light grey coloured seathearth shale with roots.
Dark mudstones with plants.

seam_marks.how: named in original: 'Coal bright cleaty ... Lower leaf of Low Main Seam (J2)'
named in original: 'Top Brass Thill Coal (K1)'
named in original: 'Position of the Bottom Brass Thill Coal, upper leaf (K2)' — core lost by reaming, position inferred
named in original: 'Botton Brass Thill Coal, lower leaf (K3)'
named in original: 'Bright cleaty coal, Hutton (L)'

notes: Modern typed IGS/BGS narrative log (Count's Corner No. 2 Borehole, drilled March 1979; cores examined by G.A.L. Johnson and G. Richardson). Lithology given as continuous depth ranges from surface with no separate Reduced Level or thickness column; depth_m = base below ground, thickness_m computed from the range. Ground level 38.25 m AOD. Depth ranges are continuous with no gaps or overlaps; all arithmetic self-consistent. Named marine-band markers (Low Main Shell Bed at 11.25 m; Brass Thill Shell Bed at intervals 8 and 11) recorded in the relevant interval annotations, not as coal seam_marks. Original typing carries several vernacular spellings preserved verbatim: 'debries' (i1), 'seathearth' (i23), 'whispy' (i19), 'Botton' (i15). Interval 12 lithology is inferred (core lost by reaming) so confidence 'probable'. Interval 1 upper part 'may be quarry debris and fill' — a possible made-ground cap over broken bedrock sandstone at the Elvet Banks quarry site; classified sandstone with a caveat. Handwritten 'NZ24SE109' and the 'BGS REGISTRATION NO. NZ 24 SE / 109 PAGE NO 1/2' stamps are archival registration marks (not seam marginalia) and were ignored per rules. Final depth 37.30 m, matching the index depth 37.3 m.
